# `breeze lock list` (default) only ever shows real file paths — same as before.
# `breeze lock list --all` additionally includes resource-kind locks (like a
# deploy claim), so "what am I holding right now" is answerable with one focused
# command instead of cross-referencing `lock list` and `inventory`, or reaching for
# the broader `operator` dashboard just to see your own holds together.
env BREEZE_DIR=$WORK/state

exec breeze identity register admin
cp stdout admin.token
exec breeze role assign deployer admin --as admin --token-file admin.token
exec breeze apply -f pipeline.hcl --as admin --token-file admin.token

exec breeze lock acquire some/file.txt --as admin --ttl 5m
exec breeze deploy claim release deploy --env staging --as admin --token-file admin.token

# Default `lock list`: file lock only, no resource claim.
exec breeze lock list --json
stdout 'some/file\.txt'
! stdout 'deploy/deploy/staging'

# `--all`: both the file lock and the resource claim appear together.
exec breeze lock list --all --json
stdout 'some/file\.txt'
stdout 'deploy/deploy/staging'
stdout '"kind":\s*"file"'
stdout '"kind":\s*"resource"'

# Plain-text output includes a Kind column under --all.
exec breeze lock list --all
stdout 'file\s+exclusive'
stdout 'resource\s+exclusive'

exec breeze stop

-- pipeline.hcl --
pipeline "release" {
  environments = ["staging"]

  stage "deploy" {
    type          = "deploy"
    fans_out      = true
    required_role = "deployer"
    timeout       = "10s"
    command       = ["/bin/true"]
  }
}
